format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is the correct H1. Sidecar reading.meta.json parses with all required fields (word_count, estimated_read_minutes, citations_used, sections). Exactly 6 sections in correct order, none added/renamed/dropped.

length_budget.notes: 1352 words is within 1200-1600. 6.76 min at 200 wpm is within the 6-8 min target.

citations.notes: All 5 primary resources cited inline. References [1]-[5] present in bibliography. No orphan markers or entries.

forward_references.notes: The cycle 1 forward_reference_overreach finding is resolved. The 14.8 block in In Practice (line 92) is now a single sentence: 'Topic 14.8 covers what to do before an agent takes an irreversible action.' This satisfies the one-sentence mention-and-defer limit. No other downstream topic references found.

sampled_assertions.assertion: A four-tier decision matrix walks you through four yes/no questions
Direct call — one prompt sent to an LLM, one response returned. No memory between calls, no external tools.
Chained calls — two or more LLM calls in sequence, output of one becomes input of next
RAG — question embedded, matching documents retrieved, injected into prompt, LLM answers
Agent — LLM uses tools and loops via Thought/Action/Observation (ReAct)
Q4 No → reconsider whether an LLM-based system is the right solution
FAQ chatbot embeds 40-item FAQ in system prompt
Order-fulfillment: receives complaint email, looks up order, checks carrier tracking, decides refund/escalate, sends reply
Prototype as a direct call; if hallucinates add RAG; if RAG not enough add agent
Over-engineering and under-engineering are both defects

faithfulness.notes: All 10 sampled assertions trace directly to corpus. No fabricated claims detected.

beginner_readability.notes: LLM spelled out as 'LLM (Large Language Model)' on first use (line 14). RAG spelled out as 'RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)' on first use (line 15). ReAct and vector database are prior-topic vocabulary (14.5, 14.6) — reuse without re-definition is permitted. No paragraph introduces 3+ brand-new terms. No hedging language detected. Sentence lengths generally under 25 words. Concrete examples and analogies (ladder analogy, capital-of-France scenario) precede abstractions throughout.

formatting.notes: Decision matrix questions rendered as structured Q1-Q4 blocks with yes/no bullet answers. Tiers introduced as a bulleted list and comparison table. Worked Example scenarios use numbered walk steps. In Practice examples separated by bold headers. All definitions use bold-term / plain-meaning pattern. No prose block exceeds 5 sentences. The 14.8 note is now a single sentence — no wall-of-text concern.

assessment_reference.notes: No mention of assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights anywhere in the reading.

scope.notes: Cycle 1 scope_creep hard finding is resolved. Line 92 now reads: 'Topic 14.8 covers what to do before an agent takes an irreversible action.' This is a single bare mention-and-defer sentence. It does not bold the deferred concept, does not supply a defining clause, and does not describe trigger scenarios or mechanisms. Complies with scope_discipline mention-and-defer rules. No other out-of-scope teaching detected.

verdict: PASS (cycle 2) — scope_creep resolved: 4-sentence 14.8 block collapsed to one plain sentence. All checks pass: format, length (1352 words / 6.76 min), citations (all 5 resources), forward references, faithfulness (10/10 assertions supported), beginner readability, formatting, no assessment references, scope clean.
